ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "user_handle" TEXT /* Handle of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "tweet_date" TEXT /* Date when the tweet was posted */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL for any articles or resources referenced in the tweet */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the tweet was posted */,
  "source" TEXT /* Platform or device used to post the tweet */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "retweet_of_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet if it is a retweet */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* List of hashtags used in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_retweet_of_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("retweet_of_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for engagement metrics record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet for which the metrics are recorded */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes */,
  "replies" INTEGER /* Number of replies */,
  "total_engagements" INTEGER /* Total number of engagements */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet was viewed */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate (total_engagements / impressions) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the sentiment record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet for which sentiment is analyzed */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Score indicating the sentiment (-1 for negative, +1 for positive) */,
  "sentiment_label" TEXT /* Label for the sentiment (e.g., 'Positive', 'Negative', 'Neutral') */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user in the system */,
  "user_handle" TEXT /* Handle of the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined the platform */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user is verified */,
  "bio" TEXT /* User's bio or description */,
  "location" TEXT /* User's location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic (e.g., U.S.-Pakistan Relations) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  "parent_topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent topic if it is a subtopic */,
  "hierarchy_level" INTEGER /* Level of the topic in the hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_topics" (
  "association_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic association */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet associated with the topic */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic associated with the tweet */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* Score indicating the relevance of the tweet to the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("association_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_topics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_topics_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was created */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., 'Sentiment Analysis', 'Engagement Metrics') */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., 'Draft', 'Published') */,
  "created_by_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("created_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, Analyst) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Numeric access level for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role associated with the permission */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Specific permission granted to the role */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Numeric access level for the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity_logs" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, report_generated) */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date when the activity took place */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the activity */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user performing the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic related to the analytics */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Average sentiment score for the topic */,
  "average_retweets" REAL /* Average number of retweets for the topic */,
  "average_likes" REAL /* Average number of likes for the topic */,
  "total_tweets" INTEGER /* Total number of tweets for the topic */,
  "total_users" INTEGER /* Total number of unique users who tweeted about the topic */,
  "most_engaged_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet with the highest engagement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id")
)
